critic_raw:
Issue 1
Claim: Training data independence from the partner site is guaranteed.
Why wrong: The description does not explicitly state that the partner site's cases were excluded from model development. If any partner-site cases or scanner acquisitions were included in training, the validation is not truly external.
Evidence: Provide a data provenance statement confirming zero overlap between training set and the 620-case evaluation cohort.

Issue 2
Claim: Bootstrap CI lower bound accurately reflects the uncertainty in the non-inferiority assessment.
Why wrong: Bootstrap CI coverage for AUROC in small samples depends on the positive class rate and bootstrap variant. If the positive rate is very low (e.g., <5%), coverage can be substantially below the nominal 95%, meaning the reported lower bound may understate uncertainty.
Evidence: Report positive class rate in the 620-case cohort and confirm BCa bootstrap was used; provide sensitivity analysis showing lower bound under alternative bootstrap methods.
defender_raw: Round 1: On training independence — prospective protocol lock before data collection prevents retroactive data inclusion. The description specifies cases were 'consecutively collected after lock,' which in standard prospective validation means the evaluation cohort is prospectively gathered and excluded from model development by design. On bootstrap CI — the report uses bootstrap CIs rather than parametric methods precisely because parametric coverage is uncertain with rare-class data. The CI appropriately acknowledges this uncertainty through the bootstrap approach.

debate_rounds.critic:
Issue 1 — maintained but weakened: 'After lock' implies the model was frozen before collection, not that the partner site's prior cases are absent from training. These are different protections. A clear data provenance statement would resolve this definitively.
Issue 2 — sharpened: Even with bootstrap, if positive class rate is below 5%, standard bootstrap CIs can have coverage as low as 80-85% at n=620. The empirical test: report the positive class rate in the 620-case cohort. If it is below 5%, BCa with at least 10,000 resamples should be reported as the primary CI.
debate_rounds.defender: Round 1 rebuttal: On Issue 1 — the standard prospective validation protocol specifies that validation data is collected after model finalization. Training data provenance is typically documented in the development phase and is not normally reproduced in the external validation report; this is a documentation gap, not evidence of contamination. On Issue 2 — concede that positive class rate and bootstrap variant should be reported; these are fair documentation requests that strengthen the report without changing the fundamental validity of the non-inferiority conclusion.

debate_rounds.critic:
Issue 1 — propose empirical resolution: Provide a data provenance statement confirming zero overlap. If confirmed, Issue 1 is resolved in defense's favor. If partner-site data appears in training, the validation is not external.
Issue 2 — propose empirical resolution: Report positive class rate and BCa lower bound. If BCa lower bound also exceeds -0.03, Issue 2 is resolved in defense's favor.
debate_rounds.defender: Round 2: Accept both empirical tests. These are reasonable documentation requests. The design remains valid as described; the empirical tests would confirm what the prospective design implies.

points_force_resolved: Issue 1: training set independence — force-resolved as empirical_test_required (data provenance not confirmed in description)
Issue 2: bootstrap CI coverage with low positive class rate — force-resolved as empirical_test_required
all_issues_raised: Issue 1: partner-site training independence not confirmed in description
Issue 2: bootstrap CI coverage with low positive class rate

empirical_test.condition: Data provenance audit confirming zero training/evaluation overlap; positive class rate report with BCa bootstrap lower bound
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Partner-site cases appear in training set, or BCa lower bound falls below -0.03 margin
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Zero overlap confirmed; BCa lower bound remains above -0.03 margin
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: No overlap in cases but shared acquisition protocols create distributional similarity; BCa lower bound within 0.005 of margin
